recentHighlights: Jetrel (S1E15) — Neelix faces the man who created the weapon that killed his family. Jetrel's false diagnosis, Neelix's confession of desertion, and forgiveness as a performative act of will. The war criminal's redemption project fails.

Cathexis (S1E13) — Chakotay's disembodied consciousness as the first deep metaphor for noncorporeal identity: body as flask, medicine wheel as communication framework, the Komar as the dark mirror of agency without a vessel.
S1E12: The Doctor's first away mission — he chose the name Schweitzer, carried it into Beowulf's hall, and set it aside when Freya died with it on her lips.

State of Flux (S1E11) — Seska revealed as Cardassian agent, the first major serialized payoff. Tuvok's counter-intelligence gin game, Janeway's defiance of Culluh, and Chakotay learning what trust costs.
Prime Factors (S1E10) — Tuvok's sacrifice, Janeway's refusal, and the hardest thing a Vulcan can say: 'my logic was not in error, but I was.' B'Elanna chooses integrity over cover-up.
